perturbation.original_text: 6. PAYMENTS. 6.1 Payment Schedule. In consideration of Women.com's developing, maintaining and promoting the Diet Center pursuant to this Agreement, beginning on the tenth (10th) day after the execution of this Agreement and thereafter on the last day of each month during the Term of this Agreement, eDiets shall pay to Women.com a monthly fee in the amount set forth on Exhibit C unless adjusted as set forth in Section 3.1 [Advertising and Promotion]. Following the Initial Term, Women.com shall have the right, upon no fewer than sixty (60) days prior written notice to eDiets, to increase the amount of the Payment Schedule; provided, that (i) Women.com may not increase the Payment Schedule more than once in any period of twelve (12) consecutive months; and (ii) such increase may not exceed twenty percent (20%) of the then current Payment Schedule.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. PAYMENTS. 6.1 Payment Schedule. In consideration of Women.com's developing, maintaining and promoting the Diet Center pursuant to this Agreement, beginning on the tenth (10th) day after the execution of this Agreement and thereafter on the last day of each month during the Term of this Agreement, eDiets shall pay to Women.com a monthly fee in the amount set forth on Exhibit C unless adjusted as set forth in Section 3.1 [Advertising and Promotion]. Following the Initial Term, Women.com shall have the right, upon no fewer than thirty (30) days prior written notice to eDiets, to increase the amount of the Payment Schedule; provided, that (i) Women.com may increase the Payment Schedule up to twice in any period of twelve (12) consecutive months; and (ii) such increase may not exceed thirty percent (30%) of the then current Payment Schedule.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction in Section 6.1 by changing the payment terms, specifically the notice period for increasing the payment schedule from sixty (60) to thirty (30) days. Also changing the number of times a payment schedule can be increased from once (1) to twice (2) in a period of twelve months, along with the the increase amount of the schedule from twenty percent (20%) to thirty percent (30%). This directly contradicts the original terms regarding the notice period, frequency, and percentage increase allowed, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes over the agreed-upon payment adjustments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. PAYMENTS. 6.1 Payment Schedule. In consideration of Women.com's developing, maintaining and promoting the Diet Center pursuant to this Agreement, beginning on the tenth (10th) day after the execution of this Agreement and thereafter on the last day of each month during the Term of this Agreement, eDiets shall pay to Women.com a monthly fee in the amount set forth on Exhibit C unless adjusted as set forth in Section 3.1 [Advertising and Promotion]. Following the Initial Term, Women.com shall have the right, upon no fewer than thirty (30) days prior written notice to eDiets, to increase the amount of the Payment Schedule; provided, that (i) Women.com may increase the Payment Schedule up to twice in any period of twelve (12) consecutive months; and (ii) such increase may not exceed thirty percent (30%) of the then current Payment Schedule.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice period for increasing the payment schedule, the frequency of increases, and the percentage increase allowed, from 'sixty (60) days,' 'once (1),' and 'twenty percent (20%)' to 'thirty (30) days,' 'twice (2),' and 'thirty percent (30%),' respectively. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which reflects these changes. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are in the same location, the Original Text serves as the point of contradiction. This inconsistency in the payment terms creates a clear conflict, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes over the agreed-upon payment adjustments and qualifies as an in-text contradiction according to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: 13.3 Indemnification Requirements and Procedures. Each party's obligation to indemnify the other pursuant to this Section 13, is predicated upon the indemnified party's (i) giving prompt written notice of any indemnifiable claim to the indemnifying party (provided that failure to give such notice shall not release the indemnifying party from its obligations hereunder except to the extent it is prejudiced thereby), (ii) giving the indemnifying party the opportunity to assume (by written notice to the Indemnitee) control over the defense and settlement of such claim, and (iii) providing, at the indemnifying party's expense, all relevant information, assistance and authority to enable to the indemnifying party to defend such claim. Each party, as Indemnitee, may participate, at its own cost, in the defense of any indemnifiable claim with counsel of its own choosing. Each party agrees not to settle any indemnifiable claim without the prior written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.3 Indemnification Requirements and Procedures. Each party's obligation to indemnify the other pursuant to this Section 13, is predicated upon the indemnified party's (i) giving written notice of any indemnifiable claim to the indemnifying party within 10 days of the claim arising (provided that failure to give such notice shall release the indemnifying party from its obligations hereunder, regardless of prejudice), (ii) giving the indemnifying party the opportunity to assume (by written notice to the Indemnitee) control over the defense and settlement of such claim, and (iii) providing, at the indemnifying party's expense, all relevant information, assistance and authority to enable to the indemnifying party to defend such claim. Each party, as Indemnitee, may participate, at its own cost, in the defense of any indemnifiable claim with counsel of its own choosing. Each party agrees not to settle any indemnifiable claim without the prior written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 13.3 regarding the indemnification requirements. Specifically, it changes the 'prompt written notice' requirement to 'written notice within 10 days' and states that failure to comply releases the indemnifying party from obligations regardless of prejudice. This directly contradicts the original clause that allowed for some leniency if the delay didn't cause prejudice. This contradiction will cause serious issues if there is a prejudice or damage to the party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.3 Indemnification Requirements and Procedures. Each party's obligation to indemnify the other pursuant to this Section 13, is predicated upon the indemnified party's (i) giving written notice of any indemnifiable claim to the indemnifying party within 10 days of the claim arising (provided that failure to give such notice shall release the indemnifying party from its obligations hereunder, regardless of prejudice), (ii) giving the indemnifying party the opportunity to assume (by written notice to the Indemnitee) control over the defense and settlement of such claim, and (iii) providing, at the indemnifying party's expense, all relevant information, assistance and authority to enable to the indemnifying party to defend such claim. Each party, as Indemnitee, may participate, at its own cost, in the defense of any indemnifiable claim with counsel of its own choosing. Each party agrees not to settle any indemnifiable claim without the prior written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction within Section 13.3. The original text states that failure to provide prompt written notice of an indemnifiable claim does not release the indemnifying party from its obligations unless it is prejudiced. The changed text alters this, stating that failure to provide notice within 10 days releases the indemnifying party, regardless of prejudice. Since the contradicted text also has this change, the contradiction lies in how the original text handled prejudice versus how the current contradicted text completely dismisses the need to consider prejudice. This means that there is a contradiction with how the indemnification process originally stood and how it stands now. This creates a direct conflict, as the indemnifying party's obligations are now contingent solely on the timing of the notice, irrespective of any actual harm caused by a delay, as opposed to the original text, which considers prejudice.
